page: Microsoft Azure E80ids_v4 is an isolated, local disk-enabled virtual machine instance from the XEIDSv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The instance provides 80 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 40 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 504.0 GB of system memory, yielding 6.3 GB of RAM per vCPU, and includes 2458 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities. The CPU architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AVX-512 Foundation, and AVX-512 VNNI, alongside AES cryptography and virtualization extensions.

In benchmark evaluations, the E80ids_v4 demonstrates strong multi-core processing and memory subsystem performance. The instance achieves a high multi-core CPU score in stress-ng and a strong PassMark CPU score of 53855.93, indicating high parallel processing capacity. Memory performance is a key strength, with cached small-block read bandwidth reaching 4152.66 GB/sec and uncached large-block read bandwidth at 214.64 GB/sec. Database and in-memory operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by a PassMark database score of 22751.33 and Redis SET performance of 64.91 million operations per second. However, single-core performance remains average, and the hardware exhibits limitations in LLM text generation tasks, where it registers poor to weak throughput.

From a resource density perspective, the E80ids_v4 offers a balanced allocation of high-capacity memory and substantial local storage, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads requiring high memory throughput without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware. The combination of 80 vCPUs, 504 GB of RAM, and 2.4 TB of local disk space makes this instance highly suitable for large-scale relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, software compilation pipelines, and multi-threaded data decompression tasks. It is less optimal for single-threaded applications or real-time LLM text generation.
description: Microsoft Azure E80ids_v4 is an isolated virtual machine from the XEIDSv4 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. It delivers 80 dedicated vCPUs across 40 physical cores, 504.0 GB of RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance supports AVX-512 instructions. Benchmarks highlight strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and exceptional database execution speeds, including 64.91 million Redis SET operations per second. However, single-core execution and LLM text generation speeds are average to poor. Given its high memory density and local storage capacity, the E80ids_v4 is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, enterprise caching systems, software compilation, and parallel data processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E80ids_v4 is an isolated virtual machine featuring 80 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 504 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage, optimized for high-throughput database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E80ids_v4 is an isolated VM with 80 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 504 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage, built for database and memory-heavy tasks.
tagline: An isolated, high-memory virtual machine with dedicated Intel Xeon processors and local storage for demanding database and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 80 dedicated vCPUs and 504 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor.
2458 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.
Strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth.
Excellent fit for database operations and Redis caching layers.
Supports AVX-512 vector and neural network instructions.
Not recommended for intensive LLM text generation tasks.
